report.name: Employee Turnover Rate - Monthly Tracking Analysis

chartData.Description: Post-holiday turnover spike - seasonal pattern expected
Temporary improvement but still above target
Sharp increase due to management changes
Temporary drop but no sustained improvement
Rising again - lack of systematic solution
Year-end high - cultural and leadership issues evident

highlightsData.title: Critical Turnover Trend Analysis
highlightsData.content: Employee turnover shows alarming upward trend from 12% to 21% annually, with average of 16.25% far exceeding industry benchmark of 8-10%. Pattern shows no improvement direction and multiple peaks indicating systemic organizational issues requiring comprehensive intervention.

attachments.name: turnover_crisis_analysis.jpg
attachments.description: Critical turnover trend analysis and organizational impact assessment

highlightsData.title: Process Instability and Unpredictability
highlightsData.content: Run chart reveals highly unstable process with extreme variation (8-22% range). No predictable pattern makes workforce planning impossible. Multiple special causes evident but underlying system problems create chronic instability requiring fundamental change.

attachments.name: process_instability_documentation.jpg
attachments.description: Process instability analysis and special cause documentation

actionItems.title: Conduct Comprehensive Organizational Culture Assessment
actionItems.description: Engage external consultant to assess organizational culture, leadership effectiveness, and employee engagement. Include anonymous surveys, focus groups, and exit interview analysis to identify root causes.

actionItems.title: Implement Emergency Retention Program
actionItems.description: Deploy immediate retention measures for high-value employees. Include stay interviews, competitive compensation review, and rapid resolution of workplace issues to stop critical talent loss.

actionItems.title: Establish Monthly Turnover Monitoring System
actionItems.description: Create real-time turnover tracking with early warning indicators. Include department-level analysis, predictive analytics, and immediate escalation protocols for abnormal patterns.
